---
title: "Multiple NIC card problem with nameserver setting"
date: 2013-01-30
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by dereklai on 2013-01-30
I have 2 NIC cards in my computer - eth0 connected to my ISP and eth1 to the internal network. This computer is also my DNS server. I'm getting the wrong setting in /etc/resolv.conf. This is what it is set to:

# Generated by NetworkManager
domain localdomain
search localdomain
nameserver 192.168.11.1

I want it set to:

nameserver 127.0.0.1  
search duidui-international.com

What is wrong with my /etc/network/interfaces file setting:

auto lo
iface lo inet loopback

auto dsl-provider
iface dsl-provider inet ppp
pre-up /sbin/ifconfig eth0 up # line maintained by pppoeconf
provider dsl-provider

auto eth0
iface eth0 inet manual

auto eth1
iface eth1 inet static
address 192.168.11.135
netmask 255.255.255.0
network 192.168.11.0
broadcast 192.168.11.255
gateway 192.168.11.1
dns-nameservers 127.0.0.1
dns-search duidui-international.com

Here's the output out my ifconfig -a:

eth0      Link encap:Ethernet  HWaddr 08:00:27:fe:d3:c2
